SEARCH
Package board

Class Board

java.lang.Object
board.Board
public class Board extends Object
Represents the 8x8 chessboard and tracks captured pieces. Responsibilities: holds the grid of Piece objects, enforces bounds checks, applies moves, and renders the board to the CLI. Check and checkmate detection are stubbed out for Phase 1.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Board()
    Creates a new board and places all pieces in their standard starting positions

    Returns true if the square holds a piece belonging to the opponent of the given color

    Returns true if the position falls within the 8x8 grid (row and column both in [0, 7])

    Attempts to move a piece from 'from' to 'to' for the given player color.

  • Constructor Details

    • Board

      public Board()
      Creates a new board and places all pieces in their standard starting positions

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public final void initialize()
      Resets the board to the standard chess starting position. Clears all squares and the captured-piece history, then places the back ranks (rows 0 and 7) and pawn ranks (rows 1 and 6). Called automatically by the constructor; can be called again to restart.

    • getPiece

      public Piece getPiece(Position position)
      Returns the piece at the given position, or null if the square is empty or out of bounds

    • isWithinBounds

      public boolean isWithinBounds(Position position)
      Returns true if the position falls within the 8x8 grid (row and column both in [0, 7])

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty(Position position)
      Returns true if the position is on the board and holds no piece

    • isOpponentPiece

      public boolean isOpponentPiece(Position position, Color color)
      Returns true if the square holds a piece belonging to the opponent of the given color

    • getCapturedPieces

      public List<Piece> getCapturedPieces()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of captured pieces in the order they were captured.
      Returns:
      The unmodifiable list.

    • movePiece

      public boolean movePiece(Position from, Position to, Color turn)
      Attempts to move a piece from 'from' to 'to' for the given player color. The move is rejected if: - either position is out of bounds - the source square is empty or holds an opponent's piece - the destination is not in the piece's legal-move list If a capture occurs, the displaced piece is added to capturedPieces. Returns true if the move was executed successfully.

    • display

      public void display()
      Prints the current board to standard output with file labels (A-H) and rank labels (1-8) on all four sides. Empty squares are shown as "##"; occupied squares use the piece's display code. AI: [Prompt] asked for a readable CLI board layout with rank and file labels; [Adopt] used the printf-based column loop and rank-inversion (rank = 8 - row); [Change] replaced the suggested "." empty-square symbol with "##" to match project style.

    • isCheck

      public boolean isCheck(Color color)
      Phase 1 stub - always returns false. Full check detection (scanning opponent attack lines) will be added in Phase 2.

    • isCheckmate

      public boolean isCheckmate(Color color)
      Phase 1 stub - always returns false. Full checkmate detection depends on isCheck() and will be added in Phase 2.
